kept.text:
This week we're demonstrating our electric air taxi over New York City. Behind every flight is a question regulators, controllers, and the public are right to ask: how does this work alongside legacy traffic in the world's busiest airspace?

In a new post, our Airspace Engineer Eric Mueller shares how we've been building the answer for years, alongside the @FAANews, @NASA, @NATCA, @USDOT and airport authorities.

The blog covers:

- Why high-tempo vertical lift operations already have strong operational precedent.

- What our acquisition of @flybladenow's passenger business taught us about moving tens of thousands of passengers through complex urban airspace.

- How human-in-the-loop simulations at the FAA's William J. Hughes Technical Center tested vertiport configurations at Orlando International Airport.

- How this work translates into the FAA's eVTOL Integration Pilot Program, where Joby is an industry partner.

Read Eric's full post ⬇️

quoted_tweet.text:
Earlier this year, we completed a series of simulations alongside NASA where we integrated up to 120 eVTOL operations per hour into the complex, busy airspace of Dallas Fort-Worth — alongside existing airport traffic.

The simulations took place at NASA's Future Flight Central, a high-fidelity virtual tower offering a 360-degree view of a real-time simulation, and involved NASA and Joby airspace engineers as well as pilots and air traffic controllers.

https://t.co/HOojDjIFzw
